title: Bridge Essay: The "common" experience of voice-hearing and its relationship with shame and guilt: a systematic review.
concept: Voice-hearing — auditory verbal hallucinations on a continuum, inner speech misattribution, source monitoring, corollary discharge.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Daimon** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Voice-hearing — auditory verbal hallucinations on a continuum, inner speech misattribution, source monitoring, corollary discharge.

**The "common" experience of voice-hearing and its relationship with shame and guilt: a systematic review.** - BMC Psychiatry (2022). DOI: 10.1186/s12888-022-03902-6. PMID: 35443637. BACKGROUND: Despite Auditory Verbal Hallucinations (AVHs) having been long associated with mental illness, they represent a common experience also in the non-clinical population, yet do not exhibit distress or need for care. Shame and guilt are emotions related to one's perception of oneself and one's responsibility. As such, they direct our attention to aspects of AVHs that are under-researched and elusive, particularly about the status of voices as others, their social implications and the con
